summary.intent: Address five PR review comments on the external-agents crate in the moltis repository (PR #566).
summary.outcome: All five review comments resolved: removed unused dependency, renamed a field, added a doc comment, added tracing instrumentation, and removed a catch-all error variant. Changes compiled cleanly, passed clippy and tests, and were committed and pushed.

repo: moltis-sessions does not depend on moltis-config, so typed config enums from moltis-config cannot be used in the sessions crate without adding a heavy dependency.
The external-agents crate uses a feature-gated tracing approach (#[cfg(feature = "tracing")]) rather than an unconditional dependency.
The field previously named last_synced_message_index was renamed to synced_message_count — the rename touched types.rs and bridge.rs in external-agents and metadata.rs in sessions.

code.finding: Had an unused moltis-agents dependency that was removed.
Contained an Other(anyhow::Error) catch-all variant that was removed per review feedback.
References to last_synced_message_index updated to synced_message_count; doc comment added on external_agent_kind explaining the serialization boundary.
tracing::instrument added to key async functions, gated behind the tracing feature flag.

workflow: Pre-push validation sequence: per-crate compile → clippy → tests → fmt check → full workspace compile, before committing.
Dependency constraints between crates should be checked before attempting to reuse types across crate boundaries.

friction: One of the review comments (tracing instrumentation on registry) required two separate Edit passes because the first edit was insufficient.
The dependency question for moltis-sessions/moltis-config required reading an extra Cargo.toml before proceeding with the fixes.
open_items: The lib.rs re-exports were checked but not changed — worth confirming no downstream consumers relied on the old last_synced_message_index name through the public API.
Tracing instrumentation is feature-gated; it is not clear whether the tracing feature is enabled in CI or production builds.
